title: Mnesithea formosa
taxonomy: Mnesithea formosa is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae. The plant is further categorized under the genus Mnesithea and is native to various parts of the world, including tropical and subtropical regions. Its classification into the Poaceae family places it among the monocotyledons, which are characterized by having one seed leaf in their seeds. The exact classification and details of the species can be found in botanical databases and scientific literature.

It is said to possess antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating skin infections and wounds.
